# Vehicle Efficiency Metrics

> Tracks a vehicle's energy efficiency (Wh/km) over time by statistically deriving an efficiency factor from charging sessions and applying it to trips for trend analysis.

**Category:** Asset · **Version:** 1.0.0 · **Tags:** vehicle · efficiency · energy · ev · analytics · telemetry

## What this does

Tracks a vehicle's energy efficiency (Wh/km) over time by statistically deriving an efficiency factor from charging sessions and applying it to trips for trend analysis.

Specifies 3 acceptance outcomes that any implementation must satisfy, regardless of language or framework.

## Fields

- **vehicle_id** *(text, required)* — Vehicle ID
- **efficiency_wh_per_km** *(number, optional)* — Efficiency (Wh/km)
- **efficiency_sample_count** *(number, optional)* — Confirmed Sample Count
- **efficiency_last_updated** *(datetime, optional)* — Efficiency Last Updated
- **preferred_range_metric** *(select, optional)* — Preferred Range Metric
- **derivation_min_sessions** *(number, optional)* — Minimum Sessions for Derivation

## What must be true

- **efficiency_from_statistical_mode:** The efficiency factor is derived by calculating energy_added_kwh / range_gained_km for each qualifying session then taking the statistical mode with sufficient samples
- **session_qualification_criteria:** A session qualifies only when duration > 10 minutes, end_battery_level <= 95%, both start and end range values are present, and charge_energy_added > 0
- **precision_fallback_strategy:** Derivation tries precision levels 5 through 2 paired with minimum sample counts 8, 5, 3, 2 — the first level with a consensus wins
- **retain_if_no_consensus:** If no precision level yields a consensus the existing factor is kept unchanged and never deleted or set to null
- **efficiency_unit:** Efficiency is expressed in Wh/km; convert to kWh/100km by dividing by 10, or to Wh/mi by dividing by 1.609
- **no_retroactive_recalculation:** Historical trips are not retroactively recalculated when efficiency changes — each trip uses the factor current at close time

## Success & failure scenarios

**✅ Success paths**

- **Efficiency Derived Successfully** — when a qualifying charging session has just completed; a consensus efficiency value found at some precision level, then Efficiency factor updated; new trips will use the improved estimate.
- **Efficiency Derivation Inconclusive** — when a qualifying charging session has just completed; no consensus found at any precision level, then Efficiency factor unchanged; previous value retained.
- **Efficiency Applied To Trip** — when a trip has just completed; efficiency_wh_per_km exists, then Energy consumed estimate attached to trip for analytics.

## Errors it can return

- `EFFICIENCY_DERIVATION_FAILED` — Could not derive an efficiency factor. More charging data required.

## Connects to

- **ev-charging-session** *(required)*
- **trip-energy-consumption** *(extends)*
- **battery-health-tracking** *(recommended)*
